title: build without seccomp
body:
If the seccomp build tag is disabled or selinux is not detected, build without
them.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
A couple of comments on this:

1. I choose to separate by function (selinux/seccomp) rather than by OS (linux, darwin, windows).  If you prefer by OS, we will need to figure out a solution for Ubuntu which is linux but no selinux.
2. I could add a seccomp.sh script to the auto part of the Make if you prefer that approach.  Currently I think it is hardcoded or overridden by TAGS=
Why do you need this?  SELinux is already isolated via the selinux code? Seems you are just making this more complicated?

I think we need this to be able to eliminate a dependency on the libseccomp shared library, at least.  If that isn't a problem we have with libselinux, I'm fine with dropping it from the PR.

LGTM either way.

When buildah is vendored into podman, selinux is not isolated and cannot be isolated when vendored in.  I'm not looking to make anything difficult.  The very fact that selinux is imported in new.go causes compiling complaints.
What is the SELinux compiler complaint?  The SELinux bindings are all Go Based, at least as far as I know.

LGTM

Although I want to create a new repository for github.com/containers/seccomp and move this out of Docker package.
the upside is if you want to pass TAGS in, this allows you to do so.

:pushpin: Commit 138d0e2 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 138d0e20ae99470a92df694cb558ac848ba08d4a -->
For reference, variables can be overridden [on the command line](https://www.gnu.org/software/make/manual/make.html#Overriding).
